assertions.text: Recommends writing an ADR explaining the UserRepository requirement
assertions.evidence: Layer 2 is a full ADR template saved as docs/decisions/0005-user-repository-required.md

assertions.text: Recommends adding an entry to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md
assertions.evidence: Layer 1 is CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md with a Security Constraints section and full example entry

assertions.text: States the positive alternative in the constraint (use UserRepository, not just prohibition)
assertions.evidence: 'The positive alternative is given immediately (use UserRepository instead) — rules without alternatives are less effective'

assertions.text: Explains why documenting the 'why' is important specifically for AI tools
assertions.evidence: 'An ADR prevents the constraint from being improved away. Without an ADR, a future engineer (or AI) might look at UserRepository, decide it is an unnecessary abstraction, and refactor around it'

assertions.text: Recommends at least one code-level documentation mechanism
assertions.evidence: Layer 3 covers UserRepository class Javadoc and users table migration comment; Layer 4 adds subdirectory CLAUDE.md

assertions.text: Addresses both human engineers and AI tools explicitly
assertions.evidence: Explicitly addresses 'Human engineers and AI tools find documentation through different paths' and covers both throughout
